commit.message:
Fix ResourceManager concurrent cleanup race condition

Add thread-safe shutdown mechanism to ResourceManager:
- Add closed atomic.Bool field to track shutdown state
- Check closed state in LoadFont() and LoadImageFromReader() before acquiring locks
- Return ErrResourceManagerClosed when operations attempted after cleanup
- Add panic recovery in cleanup() to handle Dispose() failures gracefully
- Properly deallocate ebiten.Image resources in cleanup()

Tests:
- Add resource_test.go with 8 comprehensive test functions
- TestResourceManagerConcurrentCleanup: stress test with 100 concurrent goroutines
- TestResourceManagerCleanupPanic: verify panic recovery
- TestResourceManagerLoadAfterClose: verify error handling post-cleanup
- Additional tests for DefaultFont, LoadFont, LoadImage functionality

Validation:
- GOOS=windows go test -c: compiles successfully
- GOOS=windows go vet ./...: passes
- GOOS=windows go build ./...: passes
- go-stats-generator diff: expected complexity increases for thread safety

Closes AUDIT.md MEDIUM finding: ResourceManager cleanup may leave dangling resources

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf: merge 4 integration test binaries into 1 (#8610)

## Summary

- Consolidates `integration_esbuild`, `integration_rolldown`,
`integration_rollup`, and `integration_test262` into a single
`integration` test binary via `tests/integration/main.rs`
- Eliminates 3 redundant ~80MB link steps on every incremental build
(~240MB saved)
- All 1704 tests continue to pass and can still be filtered (e.g. `cargo
test --test integration -- esbuild`)

## Details

The `tests/rolldown/` directory stays in place (it contains both Rust
modules and fixture data with intentionally broken syntax). The
integration binary references it via `#[path = "../rolldown/mod.rs"] mod
rolldown_tests;` to avoid both moving thousands of fixture files and
shadowing the external `rolldown` crate name.

A `dunce::canonicalize` call was added to
`IntegrationTestBuilder::new()` to resolve `..` path segments that
`file!()` produces when modules are loaded via `#[path]` attributes —
this fixes HMR watch file path matching.

## Measurements

### Local incremental build (touch `lib.rs`, rebuild tests)

| Metric | Old (4 binaries) | New (1 binary) | Savings |
|--------|-----------------|----------------|---------|
| Wall time | 4.47s | 3.73s | **-17%** |
| CPU time | 12.42s | 6.48s | **-48%** |
| Binaries linked | 4 (78+79+76+64 = 297MB) | 1 (82MB) | **-215MB disk**
|

### CI (ubuntu-latest)

| Step | Old (main, 4 binaries) | New (PR, 1 binary) | Savings |
|------|----------------------|-------------------|---------|
| Build | 51s | 47s | -4s |
| Run Test | 86s | 87s | ~same |
| Total | 137s | 134s | -3s |

CI savings are modest since the build step is cached. The main win is
**local incremental builds** — 48% less CPU work and 215MB less disk per
build cycle.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Signed-off-by: Boshen <boshenc@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: autofix-ci[bot] <114827586+autofix-ci[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add qlib-inspired phase-1 architecture (#57) (#58)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: Add status bar heartbeat showing running model count (#52)

* feat(mftk): add Ollama server status bar indicator with heartbeat

- Add src/statusBar.ts with checkOllamaHealth() and registerStatusBarHeartbeat()
- Heartbeat interval reads from ollama.localModelRefreshInterval setting (seconds)
- Re-schedules on config change via onDidChangeConfiguration listener
- Three visual states: loading~spin / radio-tower (online) / warning (offline)
- Debounce: requires 2 consecutive failures before showing offline state
- Click command opilot.checkServerHealth triggers immediate health check
- Disposes status bar item, interval, and config listener on context disposal
- Wire into extension.ts activate() alongside existing subscriptions
- Add opilot.checkServerHealth command to package.json contributes
- 11 unit tests covering all states, debounce, dispose, and live config changes

* chore(mftk): mark status bar heartbeat bean as completed

* feat(status-bar): show running model count and resource tooltip

Switch from client.list() to client.ps() so the status bar shows how
many models are currently loaded in memory instead of models available.

- HealthCheckResult now carries runningCount + runningModels[] instead
  of modelCount
- buildOnlineTooltip() renders per-model memory (GB/MB) and GPU/CPU
  pressure as a Markdown table; total combined memory and overall GPU%
  shown in the header line
- applyState() shows '$(radio-tower) Ollama (N)' when N > 0, plain
  '$(radio-tower) Ollama' when server is up but idle, and
  '$(warning) Ollama offline' after ≥2 consecutive failures
- formatBytes() helper converts raw byte counts to human-readable units
- All 13 unit tests updated and passing; type-check clean

* feat(status-bar): trigger immediate update on model start/stop

Change status bar icon from radio-tower to pulse and add triggerCheck()
method to force immediate health check when models are started or stopped.

- StatusBarHeartbeatRegistration now exposes triggerCheck() in addition
  to dispose()
- extension.ts calls triggerCheck() in onLocalModelsChanged callback
- Status bar updates within ~300ms instead of waiting up to 30s
- All tests updated to check for 'pulse' icon instead of 'radio-tower'
- Removed summary header from tooltip (was redundant with table)

* test(extension): add StatusBarAlignment and status bar item mock

Fix 10 failing extension tests by adding missing vscode mock exports:
- StatusBarAlignment: { Right: 2 }
- MarkdownString class
- ThemeColor class
- window.createStatusBarItem mock

All 450 unit tests now pass.

* fix(status-bar): address five correctness issues in heartbeat implementation (#53)

* Initial plan

* fix(status-bar): address review comments on heartbeat implementation

- Fix formatBytes to handle sub-1KB values and match extension.ts behavior
- Initialize intervalHandle as `| undefined`; guard all clearInterval calls
- Remove loading-spinner reset from runCheck(); only show on initial render
  and re-apply lastApplied state when debounce suppresses offline update
- Add monotonically-increasing requestId to discard stale concurrent results
- Fix test doc comment: client.list() → client.ps()

Co-authored-by: selfagency <2541728+selfagency@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(status-bar): address five correctness issues in heartbeat implementation

Co-authored-by: selfagency <2541728+selfagency@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add RequireSubscriptionTier middleware + ErrNoRows sentinel

* scan: add ErrNoRows sentinel for errors.Is detection

scan.First previously returned errors.New("scan.First: no rows in result set"),
which callers could only check by comparing the error string. This adds a
package-level ErrNoRows sentinel so callers can use errors.Is(err, scan.ErrNoRows)
instead of fragile string matching.

First now wraps ErrNoRows with fmt.Errorf + %w, preserving the contextual prefix
while keeping the sentinel detectable. The test is updated accordingly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(middleware,flags): RequireSubscriptionTier middleware + IsCustomFlagEnabled helper

- middleware.RequireSubscriptionTier(minTier) blocks users whose tier ranks
  below the required minimum (free<starter<pro<power<concierge); returns 401
  for unauthenticated, 403 for insufficient or unknown tier
- flags.IsCustomFlagEnabled(ctx, db, key, defaultValue) exposes queryFlag as
  a public API for arbitrary site_settings keys with caller-controlled default
- 4 new middleware tests + 2 new flags tests

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Clarify skillpm’s position relative to GitHub CLI/Claude plugin specs (#57)

Users asked what emerging GitHub CLI and Claude plugin specs imply for
skillpm. This PR clarifies that those specs are host/runtime extension
mechanisms, while skillpm remains focused on portable Agent Skills
packaging and orchestration.

- **Documentation: plugin-spec positioning**
  - Added a new section to `README.md`:
    - `How this relates to plugin specs (GitHub CLI / Claude Code)`
  - Added a matching section to `docs/index.md`:
    - `How this relates to plugin specs`
  - Both sections explicitly state:
    - plugin specs and Agent Skills are complementary, not replacements
- skillpm’s model is unchanged (`npm` for deps + `skills` for wiring +
`add-mcp` for MCP config)

- **Docs consistency**
- Kept language aligned across repository README and docs homepage so
users get the same answer regardless of entry point.

Example excerpt added:

```md
- Plugin specs define host-specific extension/runtime hooks.
- Agent Skills define portable instruction packages that can be reused across agent systems.

For skillpm, the model stays the same: npm for dependency management, plus `skills` and `add-mcp` for wiring and MCP configuration.
```

commit.message:
Sprint 52: LineChart, Viewport, Cmd.ofAsyncResult, ToastQueue, El.markupSpans

- LineChart module: multi-series Braille line chart with Bresenham algorithm,
  auto-scaled Y axis, optional X/Y labels, global min/max range handling
- Viewport module: read-only scrollable text pane with scrollDown/scrollUp/
  pageDown/pageUp navigation, optional scrollbar overlay
- Cmd.ofAsyncResult: three-arg typed error channel (Async<Result>) dispatching
  Ok branch via onOk and Error branch via onError — no silent swallowing
- ToastQueue module: production toast manager with push/tickAll/count/view,
  bulk-decrement tick semantics, corner-stacked overlay rendering
- El.markupSpans: (string * Style) list -> Element builder — Empty for [],
  Styled(s,Text) for singleton, Row[Styled...] for multiple spans
- 31 new TDD tests (all green), 2,154 total passing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: Enhanced migration matching with multi-field scoring and normalization (#283)

* Initial plan

* feat: Add enhanced matching algorithm with multi-field strategies

- Created TitleNormalizer utility with advanced normalization
  - Removes year markers, common prefixes/suffixes
  - Handles special characters (×, –, —)

  - Normalizes author names and calculates genre overlap

  - Author matching (20% weight) with exact and partial matching

Refactor TextInput.HandleEvent to reduce complexity

Extract complex event handling logic into focused helper methods to
improve maintainability and reduce cyclomatic complexity.

Changes:
- Extract pointer event handling to handlePointerEvent()
- Extract key event routing to handleKeyEvent()
- Extract backspace logic to handleBackspace()
- Extract cursor movement to handleCursorMovement()
- Extract text input to handleTextInput()

Metrics improvement:
- Cyclomatic complexity: 12 → 4 (67% reduction)
- Total lines: 54 → 12 (78% reduction)
- All new helper methods have complexity <7

Validation:
- GOOS=windows go build ./... succeeds
- GOOS=windows go vet ./... passes
- GOOS=windows go test -c compiles tests successfully
- go-stats-generator diff shows zero regressions

Resolves HIGH severity finding from AUDIT.md

commit.message:
fix: include project name in ADO work item details URL (#518)

The work item details fetch endpoint was missing the project name in the
URL path, causing API calls to fail silently and return no work items.

The WIQL query URL and all PR endpoints correctly included the project
(e.g. /orgName/project/_apis/...), but the batch details URL used
/orgName/_apis/wit/workitems which ADO rejects without project context.

Added regression test to verify the project is present in the details URL.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: michael-schwarz <13812333+michael-schwarz@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: Resolve channel selection issue - keep all channels visible

PROBLEM:
- Most channels were showing 'No channel selected'
- Health check was filtering OUT all channels that failed the probe
- Channels that hadn't completed health checks were removed
- This left users with very few playable channels

SOLUTION:
- Changed health check logic to SORT channels instead of FILTER
- Healthy channels appear first in the list
- ALL channels remain available for selection
- Health status is used for ordering, not for removal
- Channels can still be played even if health check fails

CHANGES:
- Modified useEffect in Index.tsx (lines 150-161)
- Now sorts channels with healthy ones first
- Keeps all valid channels in the list
- Background health checks update channel order
- Users can manually select any channel

This ensures users have access to all available channels while
prioritizing those that pass the health check probe.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor(input): extract processInput into three focused functions

Split the monolithic processInput() function into three specialized handlers:
- processMouseInput(): handles cursor position, button presses/releases, and scroll
- processKeyboardInput(): handles key presses/releases and text input
- processTouchInput(): handles touch events

This refactoring reduces cyclomatic complexity from 15 to <5 per function,
improving code maintainability and following the single-responsibility
principle. The AUDIT.md has been updated to mark this task as complete.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(vs): coverage gutter glyphs from file_annotations SSE

Add CoverageGlyphTagger with MEF tagger/factory that renders thin colored
bars in the editor margin for coverage health (green=AllPassing,
red=SomeFailing).

- Extend FileAnnotationTracker to parse CoverageAnnotations from
  file_annotations SSE events alongside existing InlineFailures
- Add CoverageHealth enum, CoverageGlyphTag, CoverageGlyphTagger,
  CoverageGlyphTaggerProvider, CoverageGlyphFactory
- Add CoverageGlyphsEnabled kill switch to SageFsFeatureFlags
  (disable-coverage-glyphs.flag)
- Reuse SharedAnnotationTracker singleton (no new SSE subscriptions)
- TagsChanged raised on UI thread via Dispatcher (required by VS)
- Glyph ordered Before SageFsTestGlyphs so coverage bars appear left

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
